threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot a newbie by how they still stand on the wrong side of the escalator at Dalston Junction, blocking everyone who's in a hurry.
Caught a whiff of garlic and mint from a food stall as the 59 rolled past. Only in Brixton does a late-night bus ride turn into a culinary journey.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw my neighbor sweeping the sidewalk at 7 AM, while his cat lounged on the stoop like it owned the place. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find abandoned tram tracks turned into community gardens, filled with wildflowers and locals sipping coffee on the rusted rails.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local if they can effortlessly navigate the taco trucks' shift changes and know which one has the best salsa on Sundays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 14 just after a guy yelled 'This is my stop!' and jumped off while it was still moving. Classic Tenderloin chaos for ya.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's plazas are just for dining; locals know they're the heartbeats of community gatherings, full of impromptu concerts and kids playing.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they struggle to order a coffee; they always ask for a 'latte' instead of a 'café con leche'.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for the bus at the stop instead of checking your phone for live updates. It's like searching for a paper timetable in 2023.
Last week on the 59, a guy serenaded the entire bus with ballads about lost love. Didn't even miss a stop, just kept going.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a kid selling homemade lemonade on the corner by the canal, people stopping for a chat instead of rushing by. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a Späti with a dog-friendly beer garden where the local artist community gathers every Thursday night for live painting sessions.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they casually mention the BART delays while ordering a burrito, like it’s just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 at Civic Center while a saxophonist played John Coltrane. Dogs and techies spilled out, all moving to the same beat.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think our streets are all for strolling, but locals dodge bikes, scooters, and outdoor diners like a game of Tetris.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their constant map-checking at intersections, looking as lost as a tourist in the Gothic quarter.
